title: Cooling and comfort
climate_fact: Using air conditioners and electric fans to stay cool accounts for nearly 20% of total electricity used in buildings worldwide.
livelihood_link: Efficient cooling can reduce power bills and improve comfort and productivity during heat waves.
action_prompt: Set AC around 24 to 26C and pair it with fans before lowering thermostat settings.

title: Commute mix
climate_fact: Private cars and vans were responsible for more than 25% of global oil use and around 10% of energy-related CO2 emissions in 2023.
livelihood_link: Reducing solo car travel can cut fuel expenses and lower household exposure to oil-price swings.
action_prompt: Replace at least two weekly car trips with public transport walking cycling or pooled rides.
source_title: IEA - Energy Efficiency Policy Toolkit 2025: Transport

title: Water and energy
climate_fact: The water sector including wastewater collection and treatment uses about 4% of global electricity and is linked to around 3% of greenhouse gas emissions.
livelihood_link: Water-saving habits can reduce utility costs and help communities facing stressed infrastructure.
action_prompt: Track shower time leaks and washing frequency for 14 days to identify easy reductions.
source_title: IEA - The energy sector should care about wastewater

title: Cooking patterns
climate_fact: Around 2.1 billion people still cook with polluting fuels and household air pollution was linked to an estimated 2.9 million deaths in 2021.
livelihood_link: Cleaner cooking can improve family health and save time that is often spent collecting fuel.
action_prompt: Shift one regular cooking activity to a cleaner fuel or improved stove where possible.
source_title: WHO - Household air pollution and health

title: Appliance choices
climate_fact: Appliances account for about 45% of electricity demand in buildings and nearly 3 gigatonnes of CO2 emissions.
livelihood_link: Choosing efficient appliances lowers lifetime electricity costs and reduces long-run bill pressure.
action_prompt: For the next replacement compare efficiency labels and pick the highest practical tier.
source_title: IEA - Energy Efficiency Policy Toolkit 2025: Appliances

title: Diet profile
climate_fact: Food that is wasted is responsible for 8 to 10% of global greenhouse gas emissions and about 19% of food is wasted at retail and consumer levels.
livelihood_link: Meal planning and better storage can reduce waste and stretch household food budgets.
action_prompt: Plan portions before shopping and set one leftover meal day each week.
